meta.purpose: Factual research data for ETA/Search Fund directory categories
meta.notes: All entries are based on web research from publicly available sources. Verify independently before publishing.

1_eta_investors_europe.description: Investors who actively invest in search funds in Europe, including institutional funds, family offices, individual investors, and accelerators/incubators.

institutional_fund_of_funds.name: Istria Capital
institutional_fund_of_funds.description: Europe's leading fund of search funds. Founded by Simon Bores and Ignacio Olavarria (also founders of Search Fund Spain). Istria I was a EUR 20M fund; Istria II closed oversubscribed at EUR 50M.
institutional_fund_of_funds.track_record: Partnered with 120+ entrepreneurs; invested in 42 operating companies. In 2023, invested in 26 search funds (23 in Europe), average ticket ~EUR 60K.
institutional_fund_of_funds.typical_ticket: ~EUR 60K per search fund (pre-dilution ~11.7% of cap table)

institutional_fund_of_funds.name: Relay Investments
institutional_fund_of_funds.geography_focus: Global (active in Europe, US, Latin America)
institutional_fund_of_funds.description: One of the few firms fully devoted to the search fund model. Actively mentors searchers pre- and post-close, offers operational support, succession planning, and collaborative community.

institutional_fund_of_funds.name: ALZA Capital Partners
institutional_fund_of_funds.description: Global fund of search funds launched in July 2018. Total commitments of USD 50M. Co-founded by a former search fund entrepreneur and pioneer of the search fund industry in Mexico and Latin America.
institutional_fund_of_funds.typical_ticket: Invests in both search and acquisition phases

institutional_fund_of_funds.name: Ambit Partners
institutional_fund_of_funds.description: Dedicated search fund investor enabling ETA in traditional and developing markets. Founded by Rob LeBlanc. Closed USD 26M fund.
institutional_fund_of_funds.track_record: Invested in ~80 search funds across 30 countries.

institutional_fund_of_funds.name: Vonzeo Capital
institutional_fund_of_funds.description: First fund investing in search funds established outside the United States. First globally diversified SF portfolio. Team members have exited searches, created startups, and invested in searches. Led by Jan Simon (also IESE Academic Director).

institutional_fund_of_funds.name: Innesto Partners
institutional_fund_of_funds.geography_focus: Europe (France, Italy, Netherlands, Spain, Germany, UK) and North America
institutional_fund_of_funds.description: Belgian private equity fund founded in 2021, dedicated to search fund investing with a focus on Europe. Among the first funds focused on actively finding and investing in European search fund opportunities.

institutional_fund_of_funds.name: Beka Alpha Search Funds
institutional_fund_of_funds.geography_focus: Spain, Portugal, Italy, rest of Europe
institutional_fund_of_funds.description: Vehicle created by entrepreneurs that pools capital to identify, buy and manage SMEs. Target size EUR 20M. Minimum investment EUR 100K. 4-year investment period. As of Oct 2024, EUR 12M AUM with portfolio sales exceeding EUR 100M.

institutional_fund_of_funds.name: Search Capital Partners
institutional_fund_of_funds.description: Created by entrepreneurs with years of expertise in M&A, management, and direct management of search funds. Provides capital, mentorship, and strategic networks for scale-up.

institutional_fund_of_funds.name: 9T Capital
institutional_fund_of_funds.description: Investment firm singularly focused on partnering with exceptional entrepreneurs to build enduring companies through the ETA/Search Fund model.

institutional_fund_of_funds.name: Newton Equity Partners
institutional_fund_of_funds.description: Bridges entrepreneurship and private equity, supporting responsible ownership, sustainable growth, and resilient long-term returns. Also runs the Search Fund Institute with library and resources.

institutional_fund_of_funds.name: Scipio Holding
institutional_fund_of_funds.description: Holding company investing in international SMEs, specialized in the search fund model. In 2024, partnered with Swanlaab Venture Factory to launch a EUR 25M fund.

institutional_fund_of_funds.name: Search Fund Club (Italy)
institutional_fund_of_funds.description: Club deal investing in European search funds, launched by an Italian team who successfully launched 4 SPACs in Italy and one in the Netherlands.

institutional_fund_of_funds.name: ONEtoONE Corporate Finance / Asset Management
institutional_fund_of_funds.geography_focus: Spain, Portugal, France, Italy, UK, DACH region
institutional_fund_of_funds.description: Actively looking to invest in search funds in Europe with special focus on Iberia, France, Italy, UK and DACH.

institutional_fund_of_funds.name: SearchRight
institutional_fund_of_funds.description: Investing in the future of SMEs. European search fund investment platform.

operator_led_buyout_platforms.name: Novastone Capital Advisors / Novastone Partners
operator_led_buyout_platforms.hq: Global (active in Europe and North America)
operator_led_buyout_platforms.description: Global firm solving succession challenges for SMEs through its Operator-Led Buyout Program. Finds exceptional operators first, then identifies acquisition targets together. Syndicates each acquisition deal-by-deal from HNWIs, family offices, and funds.
operator_led_buyout_platforms.track_record: Backed ~75 operators, closed 24 acquisitions since 2020. USD 455M+ enterprise value across 26 portfolio companies. Recently launched ETA Hub in Germany with UnternehmerTUM, Tengelmann Growth Partners, and Deutsche Bank.

operator_led_buyout_platforms.name: Chapters Group
operator_led_buyout_platforms.description: Publicly listed decentralized investment holding focused on acquiring and developing smaller, asset-light niche companies with resilient business models. Long-term, owner-led approach. Leadership: Jan-Hendrik Mohr (CEO), Marlene Carl (CFO), Marc Maurer (COO). Backed by Antheia (Daniel Ek's family office), MIT endowment, Sator Grove, Mitch Rales (Danaher co-founder).

operator_led_buyout_platforms.name: Seqos Capital
operator_led_buyout_platforms.description: Works with select management entrepreneurs to find and acquire excellent companies and manage them as equity-owning CEOs. New entrant in 2022, offers platform for searchers in DACH region.

operator_led_buyout_platforms.name: Tembo Search Partners
operator_led_buyout_platforms.description: Facilitates succession planning in the SME sector by connecting search fund entrepreneurs, investors, and business owners. Primarily active in DACH region.

operator_led_buyout_platforms.name: WAD Capital
operator_led_buyout_platforms.description: ETA accelerator focused on acquiring and growing SMEs with EUR 1-5M EBITDA. Uses data-driven approach and cohort model to empower CEOs to lead businesses through succession phases.

uk_focused_investors.name: Ryton Equity
uk_focused_investors.description: Backs talented individuals looking to acquire and grow businesses. Long-term partners with extensive experience of search, acquisition and operations.

uk_focused_investors.name: Kortica Capital
uk_focused_investors.description: UK-based search fund led by one entrepreneur and supported by experienced operators and hands-on individuals. Seeks to acquire, actively manage, and grow one privately-held UK business.

uk_focused_investors.name: Sentinel Capital (Search Funds division)
uk_focused_investors.description: Active investor providing entrepreneurs with support across all stages of the search fund experience. Seasoned veterans with over 60 years of owner/operator experience.

spain_focused_investors.name: Sachem Partners
spain_focused_investors.description: Privately-held investment vehicle formed to identify, acquire and operate existing private companies in Spain, with objective of long-term sustainable growth. Linked to Kinderhook Partners.

spain_focused_investors.name: Cabiedes & Partners
spain_focused_investors.type: Venture Capital (also active in search funds)
spain_focused_investors.description: Spanish venture capital firm founded in 2009. Provides seed, early-stage, and later-stage investment services. Also involved in the search fund ecosystem.

latin_america_active_in_europe.name: Bridge Capital
latin_america_active_in_europe.description: Founded by an MBA from Stanford GSB. Active in the search fund space, primarily in Latin America.

us_investors_active_in_europe.name: Pacific Lake Partners
us_investors_active_in_europe.description: Committed private investment fund founded 2009. USD 250M of committed capital. Invested in over 100 operating companies. Team of 32 including 14 partners. Sole mandate: investments in search fund entrepreneur-led companies.

us_investors_active_in_europe.name: ETA Equity
us_investors_active_in_europe.description: Founded by Wharton graduates Mark Sinatra and Matthew Zucker. Matt has invested in search funds since 2008, in over 90 search funds and over 30 search-acquired businesses.

us_investors_active_in_europe.name: Anacapa Partners
us_investors_active_in_europe.description: One of the earliest institutional search fund investors, founded 2010. Focused solely on search. Strong reputation for follow-on capital and board support.

us_investors_active_in_europe.name: Peterson Partners
us_investors_active_in_europe.description: Backs strong leaders and creative thinkers with tailored investment options. Strong brand in the mid-market.

us_investors_active_in_europe.name: M2O Search / M2O Inc.
us_investors_active_in_europe.type: Family Investment Office / Search Fund Investor
us_investors_active_in_europe.description: Los Angeles-based family investment office investing in search funds for 30+ years. Targets service-based businesses with USD 1M-15M EBITDA. Combines capital with executive search services.

us_investors_active_in_europe.name: TTCER Partners
us_investors_active_in_europe.description: Search Fund Alliance member. Known for growth-focused partnership approach.

us_investors_active_in_europe.name: Housatonic Partners
us_investors_active_in_europe.type: Private Equity / Early Search Fund Backer
us_investors_active_in_europe.description: Private equity firm that backed many of the original search funds. One of the earliest institutional participants.

us_investors_active_in_europe.name: Milk Street Ventures
us_investors_active_in_europe.description: Seasoned investors in search funds helping young entrepreneurs transition successfully to become CEOs.

us_investors_active_in_europe.name: Aspect Investors
us_investors_active_in_europe.description: Active search fund investor. Search Fund Alliance member.

us_investors_active_in_europe.name: Smash.vc
us_investors_active_in_europe.description: Top investor in self-funded search funds and independent sponsor transactions. Equity checks USD 250K-1.5M, minority positions. Also provides in-house digital marketing agency support for portfolio companies. Targets businesses with USD 750K-5M EBITDA.

us_investors_active_in_europe.name: CapitalPad
us_investors_active_in_europe.description: Built for self-funded searchers and small deal sizes. Provides USD 500K-2M direct capital for US-based transactions.

us_investors_active_in_europe.name: Liberty Partners / Liberty Search Ventures
us_investors_active_in_europe.description: Family office with interest in succession deals. Focuses on healthcare, B2B services, financial services. Targets companies with USD 500K-5M EBITDA, invests USD 500K-1.5M per traditional search fund.

us_investors_active_in_europe.name: Search Fund Partners
us_investors_active_in_europe.description: Led by ex-searchers. Dedicated to search fund investment.

us_investors_active_in_europe.name: Next Coast ETA
us_investors_active_in_europe.description: Supports both traditional and self-funded searchers. Offers capital, deal sourcing help, and executive recruiting post-close.

us_investors_active_in_europe.name: Red Forest Capital
us_investors_active_in_europe.description: Focused on backing first-time searchers with clear theses and operational potential.

us_investors_active_in_europe.name: The Nashton Company
us_investors_active_in_europe.description: Prioritizes fit between searcher and business. Looks for gritty, execution-focused operators. Stays involved post-close.

us_investors_active_in_europe.name: The Cambria Group
us_investors_active_in_europe.type: Lower Middle Market / Search Fund Investor
us_investors_active_in_europe.description: Invests across the lower middle market. Occasionally backs searchers where acquisition fits existing portfolio themes.

us_investors_active_in_europe.name: Broadtree Partners
us_investors_active_in_europe.description: Combines private equity model with mentoring style of an accelerator. Helps entrepreneurs acquire and operate lower-middle-market companies. Co-invests and co-operates.

search_fund_alliance_members.description: Non-profit organization of search fund investors and experienced CEOs, founded 2016.

accelerators_incubators.name: Search Fund Accelerator (SFA)
accelerators_incubators.description: First and most recognized accelerator for search fund entrepreneurs. Founded by Timothy Bovard in 2015. Cohort-based program. Reports 88% acquisition success rate. Active globally.

accelerators_incubators.name: Novastone ETA Hub (with UnternehmerTUM)
accelerators_incubators.description: Germany's first dedicated ETA platform. Partnership between Novastone Partners, UnternehmerTUM, Tengelmann Growth Partners, and Deutsche Bank WM. Promotes succession solutions in mid-market through operator-led buyouts.

accelerators_incubators.name: Acquisition Lab
accelerators_incubators.description: Founded by Walker Deibel (author of Buy Then Build). Has closed almost 200 deals worth over USD 350M. Provides structure, standards, and community for operators. Offers aligned funding for qualified operators.

accelerators_incubators.name: Trilogy Search Partners
accelerators_incubators.description: Works closely with search fund entrepreneurs, offering funding and ongoing operational mentorship.

accelerators_incubators.name: NextGen Growth Partners
accelerators_incubators.description: Combines search fund principles with institutional investment backing. Integrates operational strategy and data-driven acquisition support.

accelerators_incubators.name: Halstatt Legacy Partners
accelerators_incubators.description: Supports search fund entrepreneurs by combining private capital with long-term partnership. Focuses on lower-middle-market companies.

accelerators_incubators.name: Pathfinder Group
accelerators_incubators.description: Offers structured mentorship, leadership workshops, and deal support for searchers. Emphasizes clarity, discipline, and leadership.

1_eta_investors_europe.notes: The European search fund market is growing rapidly. UK is the most active market, followed closely by Spain. Italy and France are approximately 6-8 years behind Spain in development. Germany shows strong potential with ~130,000 SMEs needing succession. A typical European search fund brings together about a dozen investors, each with a minority stake, for an average initial investment of EUR 25,000 per investor.

2_mba_programs_teaching_eta.description: Business schools with formal ETA/search fund courses, programs, clubs, or significant resources.

us_programs.program_details: Pioneer of search fund education. Short course launched 2009 by Peter Kelly and David Dodson. David Dodson wrote original course material for search funds. Stanford's Center for Entrepreneurial Studies (CES) publishes the biennial Search Fund Study (latest: 2024, covering 681 qualifying search funds). Also runs the Search Fund CEO Conference.
key_professors: Sara Heston (Assistant Director, Search Fund Project)

us_programs.program_details: Royce Yudkoff and Richard Ruback started teaching ETA ~15 years ago. Two courses: (1) 'The Financial Management of Smaller Firms' (fall, case-based), (2) 'Entrepreneurship through Acquisition' (spring, field). Also offers the Search Fund Fellowship (USD 65,000/year for graduating students to pursue ETA search). Produces the 'Think Big, Buy Small' podcast.
key_professors: Richard S. Ruback (Baker Foundation Professor, Willard Prescott Smith Professor of Corporate Finance)
Royce Yudkoff (Professor of Management Practice of Entrepreneurial Management)

us_programs.school: University of Chicago Booth School of Business
us_programs.program_details: Credit-bearing ETA course offered for Full-Time and Evening/Weekend MBA students. Co-hosts the annual Booth-Kellogg ETA Conference. Runs the ETA Insider Podcast. Has emerged as a leading university for ETA training with conference, podcast, and dedicated class.
key_professors: Brian O'Connor (Adjunct Professor of Entrepreneurship, also Managing Partner of NextGen Growth Partners)

us_programs.program_details: Co-hosts the annual Booth-Kellogg ETA Conference with Chicago Booth. Has ETA-focused resources and student clubs.
Three ETA courses: MGMT 2130 (undergrad: ETA), MGMT 8110 (MBA: ETA), MGMT 8140 (MBA: Search Fund Entrepreneurship). VentureLab ETA program. Hosts the Wharton ETA Summit.

us_programs.school: University of Virginia Darden School of Business
us_programs.program_details: Batten Institute supports ETA students with elective courses, independent studies, faculty mentorship, ETA student club, internship support, and speakers. Co-hosts the Southeast ETA Conference (SEETA).

us_programs.school: Duke University Fuqua School of Business
us_programs.program_details: ETA class covering the entire process: funding models, search, negotiation, first 100 days, and exit. Co-hosts SEETA conference.

Offers search fund/ETA class. Published case studies: 'How to Prepare for a Search Fund During Your MBA' (2020), 'A Reference Guide on European Search Funds' (2023), 'Exploring Various Search Fund Structures' (2021).
ETA@MIT Club organizes events and the annual ETA@MIT Summit (10th anniversary edition in February 2026). Active student-led ETA community.

us_programs.school: Carnegie Mellon Tepper School of Business
us_programs.program_details: 4-course ETA track: (1) Entrepreneurial Alternatives, (2) ETA Survey, (3) ETA Workshop, (4) Designing and Leading a Business Capstone. Takes students from first hearing about search to launching their own search.
ETA program/workshop and 'Private Equity Practicum' seminar focused on small market investment.
Hosts flagship Southern California ETA conference. Active Entrepreneur Association with ETA focus.
Hosts annual ETA conference (February). Strong entrepreneurship focus with ETA curriculum.
ETA club and coursework. Active in the broader ETA conference circuit.
Offers 'Entrepreneurship Through Acquisition (ETA)' course through Professional & Continuing Education, accessible beyond MBA enrollment.

us_programs.school: University of Calgary Haskayne School of Business

europe_programs.program_details: Home to the International Search Fund Center (since 2011). Hosts the biennial International Search Fund Conference (since 2015, Barcelona). Works in conjunction with Stanford GSB to track and publish biennial reports on international search funds. Jan Simon is Academic Director. Entrepreneurial environment supported by Summer Entrepreneurship Experience, Business Angels Network, and Finaves VC Fund.
key_professors: Jan Simon (Managing Partner of Vonzeo Capital, Academic Director of International Search Fund Center)

europe_programs.program_details: Courses focused on search funds and ETA covering key steps: raising a fund, acquiring and managing a business, evaluation, due diligence, acquisition financing. INSEAD Search Fund & ETA Club. Hosts INSEAD ETA Conference (twice annually starting 2026: May in Fontainebleau, November in Singapore). First conference held Singapore Nov 2025 with 250+ attendees.
ETA elective available through MBA, EMBA, and Sloan programmes. Equips students with knowledge and skills for starting ETA journey. Part of the Institute of Entrepreneurship and Private Capital.

europe_programs.school: RSM Erasmus University (Rotterdam School of Management)
europe_programs.program_details: Hosts Inaugural RSM ETA Conference focused on the Benelux region. Connects leading investors, operators, search fund entrepreneurs, MBA students and alumni.
16-month MBA program. Known to have ETA-related coursework and student interest groups.
Strong entrepreneurship focus with ETA interest. Part of the active Spanish ETA ecosystem.
Barcelona-based. Part of the active Spanish search fund ecosystem.
Known ETA activity and student interest in search funds.
Growing ETA interest. Approximately 320 MBA students annually.

europe_programs.school: WHU - Otto Beisheim School of Management
europe_programs.program_details: German school with ETA coursework. Part of the growing German ETA ecosystem.
Top-ranked European business school. Growing ETA interest in Italian market.
Swiss school with customizable approach. ETA interest growing in Swiss market.
Leading European school. Strategic thinking and international business focus relevant to ETA.
Top-ranked German program with growing ETA interest.

asia_pacific.program_details: Maintains comprehensive list of search fund courses worldwide at smeventures.com/insights/search-fund-courses. Tracks ETA programs globally.

2_mba_programs_teaching_eta.notes: Most ETA programs require full MBA enrollment. A comprehensive, maintained list of search fund courses worldwide is available at smeventures.com. The Stanford Search Fund Primer and the IESE International Search Fund Study are the two most important academic publications in the field.

3_ma_brokers_sme.category: M&A Brokers Specialized in SME (by country)
3_ma_brokers_sme.description: Brokers, marketplaces, and intermediaries specialized in small/medium business acquisitions.

united_kingdom.name: BusinessesForSale.com
united_kingdom.description: World's largest marketplace with 58,748+ businesses for sale in 130+ countries. Started mid-1990s. UK's No. 1 for business sales.

united_kingdom.name: Daltons Business
united_kingdom.description: UK's leading website for buying/selling businesses. Launched 2002 from Daltons Weekly newspaper (published 1867-2011). 20,000+ businesses listed; ~90,000 business buyers visit weekly.

united_kingdom.name: Christie & Co
united_kingdom.description: Largest team of sector specialists in UK. Largest group of hotel specialists in Europe. Professional brokerage and advisory from offices across UK and Europe.

united_kingdom.name: Business Sale Report (BSR)
united_kingdom.description: Leading UK businesses for sale marketplace.

france.name: CRA (Cedants et Repreneurs d'Affaires)
france.description: National non-profit created 1985. Facilitates sale/transmission of SMEs with 3-100 employees. 240 delegates across 73 delegations nationally. ~800 qualified SMEs for sale and 1,500 qualified buyers at any time. ~600 sellers/year, 1,200 buyers. 13,000+ businesses entrusted since founding. 1 in 3 businesses in portfolio results in a sale.

france.name: Bpifrance Transmission
france.description: Bpifrance's dedicated platform for business transmission and acquisition. Partners with CRA and other operators.

france.name: Transentreprise
france.description: National platform backed by chambers of commerce and industry and chambers of trades/crafts. 6,000+ sale offers. Covers 98 departments in France.

france.name: Cession PME
france.description: Leading French site for sale and acquisition of businesses, shops and franchises. 118,000+ recent listings.

france.name: Infogreffe Marketplace
france.description: Partnership with CRA. Official business registry data combined with transmission marketplace.

germany.name: nexxt-change
germany.description: Germany's largest exchange for business succession. Free for both sides. Offered by Federal Ministry for Economics with KfW Banking Group, DIHK, Sparkassen. ~800 regional partners. 1,000+ successful successions annually.

germany.name: DUB.de (Deutsche Unternehmerborse)
germany.description: Most far-reaching independent online platform for business transactions in Germany since 2011. ~80,000 registered users. ~3,500 listings. Paid listings (several hundred euros).

germany.name: KERN - Unternehmensnachfolge
germany.description: One of the largest consultant groups of succession specialists in Germany, Austria, Switzerland, and Europe. 28 locations. Focuses on business sales, generational change, and purchases.

germany.name: Carl Finance
germany.description: Founded 2016. Professional sales support for mid-market businesses. Network of 160 partner consultants. Provides active support (unlike nexxt-change or DUB which are passive marketplaces).

germany.name: Pro-Consult
germany.description: SME business broker and M&A consultant for 25+ years. Cross-border capabilities with specialized local representatives across Europe.

spain.name: BusinessesForSale.com Spain
spain.description: 58,610+ businesses for sale in Spain from top brokers and independent sellers.

spain.name: Business Broker Spain
spain.description: Premier business agent offering the largest selection of businesses for sale in Spain.

spain.name: Direct Business Brokers (Spain)
spain.description: Helps buyers find business opportunities in Spain.

italy.name: Consulting Italia Group SRL
italy.description: Italian firm specializing in cessione (sale), vendita (sale) and acquisto (purchase) of businesses.

italy.name: Synergy Business Brokers (Italy operations)
italy.description: Has buyers for companies in Italy among other European countries.

italy.name: SMERGERS (Italy)
italy.description: First online investment bank for SMEs. Lists active business brokers in Italy.

netherlands.name: Brookz
netherlands.description: Platform for business acquisitions in the Netherlands. 1,200+ businesses for sale.

netherlands.name: Bedrijventekoop.nl
netherlands.description: Platform for businesses for sale in the Netherlands.

netherlands.name: Company Brokers
netherlands.description: Specialist in bedrijfsovernames (business acquisitions). Operates in the Netherlands and internationally.

netherlands.name: MaeS en Partners
netherlands.description: ~25 transactions annually. Guided 300+ acquisitions over 25+ year history.

switzerland.name: Companymarket
switzerland.description: Largest succession and business brokerage portal in Switzerland. Originally launched 2007 as NEXTmarket. 1M+ page views, 1,000+ active search subscriptions per year.

switzerland.name: Business Broker AG
switzerland.description: 15+ years specializing in company sales and succession for Swiss SMEs. Database with several hundred companies for sale. All sectors.

switzerland.name: OBT
switzerland.description: Swiss advisory firm offering buying and selling company services.

switzerland.name: TCFG
switzerland.description: Mergers & Acquisitions and Corporate Finance consulting. Specializes in entrepreneurial successions.

switzerland.name: Swiss AMF AG
switzerland.description: Leading Swiss corporate administration services provider with M&A business broker services.

nordics.name: Dealsuite (Nordics)
nordics.description: Leading platform for M&A transactions. 121 Nordic M&A advisory firms contributed expertise. Detailed data on Nordic mid-market (EUR 1M-200M revenue).

nordics.name: Lincoln International (Nordics)
nordics.description: Nordic office originated as regional M&A boutique in Stockholm, joined Lincoln in 2018. Serves Sweden, Norway, Denmark, Finland.

nordics.name: Yrityskaupat.net
nordics.description: Finnish platform for businesses for sale (Foretag till salu).

nordics.name: Sunbelt Business Brokers (Norway)
nordics.description: Part of global Sunbelt network, serving Norwegian market.

united_states.name: BizBuySell
united_states.description: World's largest proprietary database of businesses for sale. Wide variety of industries and sizes from USD 50K to USD 50M.

united_states.name: Sunbelt Business Brokers
united_states.description: Since 1978. Claims to have sold more businesses than any broker in the world. 1,000+ brokers worldwide. Largest proprietary database. #1 firm per IBBA for two years running. Annual revenue USD 500K-50M.

united_states.name: Murphy Business & Financial Services
united_states.description: Offices in 36 US states plus Canada. 25 years in business. High closing ratio. Brokerage, valuations, M&A, appraisals, commercial real estate.

united_states.name: Viking Mergers & Acquisitions
united_states.description: Sold 950+ businesses since 1996. Offices across Southeast, Mid-Atlantic, and Texas.

united_states.name: DealStream
united_states.description: Leading online marketplace connecting buyers and sellers globally, including European businesses.

united_states.name: Axial
united_states.description: Platform connecting lower middle market companies with capital. Lists search funds, PE firms, and business brokers.

pan_european.name: SMERGERS
pan_european.description: First online investment bank for SMEs. 2,346+ active businesses for sale in Europe. 138+ active brokers in Europe.

pan_european.name: Synergy Business Brokers
pan_european.description: Buyers for companies across UK, Germany, Spain, Italy, Romania, France, Portugal, Belgium, Sweden, Croatia, and more.

pan_european.name: Flippa
pan_european.description: Global marketplace for buying/selling businesses. Lists top brokers by country across Europe.

pan_european.name: Clairfield International
pan_european.description: Top-10 M&A advisor in France, Germany, Spain, and Nordics. Specialist mid-market M&A network.

4_eta_conferences_events.description: Conferences, summits, and networking events related to ETA and search funds.

major_conferences.name: IESE International Search Fund Conference
major_conferences.description: Primary global gathering for traditional searchers, institutional investors, and SMB operators. Hosted at IESE's Barcelona campus. Brings together search-CEOs, active searchers, new searchers, leading academics, and search fund investors worldwide.

major_conferences.name: Stanford Search Fund CEO Conference
major_conferences.description: Focuses on search fund CEOs. Hosted by Stanford GSB's Center for Entrepreneurial Studies.

major_conferences.name: Booth-Kellogg Entrepreneurship Through Acquisition (ETA) Conference
major_conferences.next_date: March 10, 2026 at Kellogg School of Management, Evanston, IL
major_conferences.description: Annual event bringing together search fund entrepreneurs, investors, MBA students, faculty, and other ETA participants.

major_conferences.name: INSEAD ETA Conference
major_conferences.description: First edition held Singapore Nov 2025 with 250+ attendees. Starting 2026, held twice annually bringing international dimension to ETA circuit.

major_conferences.name: MIT Sloan ETA Summit (ETA@MIT)
major_conferences.description: Titled 'Breaking the Model: Reinventing the Traditional Search Fund.' Explores emerging search tools, first-100-day CEO lessons, 2026 financing trends.

major_conferences.name: Southeast Entrepreneurship Through Acquisition Conference (SEETA)
major_conferences.next_date: September 18-19, 2026 at Duke Fuqua School of Business
major_conferences.description: Co-hosted by UVA Darden, UNC Kenan-Flagler, and Duke Fuqua. Equips MBA students and professionals to pursue business acquisition. Highly regarded for accessibility to mid-career professionals.

major_conferences.name: Kellogg ETA Summit
major_conferences.description: Kellogg School of Management's dedicated ETA summit (separate from the joint Booth-Kellogg conference).

major_conferences.name: UCLA Anderson ETA Conference

major_conferences.name: RSM ETA Conference (Benelux)
major_conferences.description: Inaugural conference focused on the growing ETA landscape in the Benelux region. Hosted by RSM Erasmus University.

major_conferences.name: Latam Search Fund Conference
major_conferences.description: Second edition of the Latin American Search Fund Conference.

major_conferences.name: Bits & Pretzels ETA Track
major_conferences.description: UnternehmerTUM, Novastone, Deutsche Bank, and Tengelmann Growth Partners put ETA center stage at this major European startup/business conference.

event_directories.name: Searcher Insights Conference Directory
event_directories.description: Comprehensive list of search fund conferences with dates and registration links.

event_directories.description: Upcoming events in the investor community.

event_directories.name: Jim Stein Sharpe Conferences
event_directories.description: Comprehensive conference listing maintained by search fund expert.

5_eta_podcasts.description: Podcasts about ETA, search funds, and small business acquisition.

english_language.name: Acquiring Minds
english_language.description: Premier podcast for acquisition entrepreneurs. Interviews with people who have actually bought businesses, from BizBuySell buyers to traditional searchers. Real, actionable insights.

english_language.name: Think Like an Owner
english_language.description: Seeks conversations with small company owners and investors to learn how to acquire and run small companies. Focus on search funds, micro private equity, and permanent capital.

english_language.name: ETA Insider Podcast
english_language.host: Brian O'Connor (Adjunct Professor, Chicago Booth; Managing Partner, NextGen Growth Partners)
english_language.description: All things related to search funds and ETA. Produced by Polsky Center for Entrepreneurship at University of Chicago.

english_language.name: Think Big, Buy Small
english_language.description: HBS podcast about ETA. Season 3 features guests like Will Smith (Acquiring Minds host) and Paige Sopcic (President, CanSource). Spotlights the growing world of acquisition entrepreneurship.

english_language.name: Search Funded: The ETA Podcast
english_language.description: Interviews with acquisition entrepreneurs and investors to distill best practices for acquiring and operating established businesses.

english_language.name: INSEAD ETA and Search Funds Podcast
english_language.description: Explores the dynamic world of ETA. Episodes showcase individuals from across the ETA journey--entrepreneurs in search, operations, exit, and experienced investors.

english_language.name: The M&A Zing
english_language.description: Weekly insights for SMB M&A, search funds, HoldCo builders and acquisition entrepreneurs. Topics and challenges impacting SMB succession.

english_language.name: How2Exit: Buy, Don't Build
english_language.description: Premier podcast for entrepreneurs, advisors, and everyone in between about buying, growing, and selling small businesses valued below USD 20M. 290+ episodes over 4 years.

english_language.name: Buy and Build
english_language.description: Weekly podcast exploring nuances of buying and building small businesses. Focus on UK and Europe. Recommended for UK and European searchers.

english_language.name: Sense and Sensibility in Search Fund Universe
english_language.description: Dedicated to exploring art and science of search funds and ETA. Balances strategic/analytical side with human/intuitive elements of deal-making and relationship-building.

english_language.name: Minds Capital Podcast
english_language.description: 50+ interviews with sponsors and capital providers. Connection to the growing world of independent sponsorship and entrepreneurial private equity.

5_eta_podcasts.notes: No dedicated French or Spanish language ETA podcasts were identified in research, though some episodes of broader business podcasts may cover the topic. The INSEAD podcast covers international perspectives including European markets.

6_eta_books.description: Curated reading list of essential books for search fund entrepreneurs and investors.

core_search_fund_books.title: Search Funds & Entrepreneurial Acquisitions: The Roadmap for Buying a Business and Leading it to the Next Level
core_search_fund_books.author: Jan Simon
core_search_fund_books.description: Comprehensive and definitive guide to search funds. Best-practices-based roadmap for searchers, acquisition entrepreneurs, investors and board members. Written by IESE's Academic Director of the International Search Fund Center and Vonzeo Capital Managing Partner. 4.44/5 on Goodreads.

core_search_fund_books.title: HBR Guide to Buying a Small Business: Think Big, Buy Small, Own Your Own Company
core_search_fund_books.author: Richard S. Ruback & Royce Yudkoff
core_search_fund_books.description: Practical manual from Harvard Business Review. Covers value assessment, deal structure, pricing, terms, legal steps, tax issues, and post-acquisition operations. Written by the two HBS professors who pioneered ETA education.

core_search_fund_books.title: Buy Then Build: How Acquisition Entrepreneurs Outsmart the Startup Game
core_search_fund_books.author: Walker Deibel
core_search_fund_books.description: Cornerstone book for acquisition entrepreneurs. Positions buying a business as smarter alternative to starting from scratch. Compelling and accessible introduction to ETA. Deibel also founded Acquisition Lab.

core_search_fund_books.title: Stanford Search Fund Primer
core_search_fund_books.author: Stanford Graduate School of Business (various authors)
core_search_fund_books.description: The most important foundational resource in the search fund community. Free publication from Stanford GSB covering the search fund model, economics, and process.

core_search_fund_books.title: 2024 Search Fund Study
core_search_fund_books.author: Peter Kelly, Sara Heston (Stanford GSB)
core_search_fund_books.description: Biennial study analyzing 681 qualifying search funds in US and Canada. Found aggregate pre-tax IRR of 35.1% and 4.5x ROIC. The gold-standard data source for search fund performance.

acquisition_process_books.title: The Art of Buying a Business
acquisition_process_books.author: Richard Parker
acquisition_process_books.description: Straightforward advice and strategies for navigating the business-buying process. Ideal for first-time buyers looking for a clear roadmap.

acquisition_process_books.title: Buying a Business That Makes You Rich
acquisition_process_books.author: John Martinka
acquisition_process_books.description: Recommended by the ETA community. Practical guide to finding and buying the right business.

acquisition_process_books.title: How to Buy a Business without Being Had
acquisition_process_books.author: Jack (John) Chmielewski
acquisition_process_books.description: Popular in the ETA community for practical negotiation and due diligence advice.

acquisition_process_books.title: The Messy Marketplace: Selling Your Business in a World of Imperfect Buyers
acquisition_process_books.author: Brent Beshore
acquisition_process_books.description: Insight into how sellers think, what motivates them, and how to approach acquisitions with seller perspective in mind. Helps acquisition entrepreneurs understand the other side of the deal.

acquisition_process_books.title: The Private Equity Playbook
acquisition_process_books.author: Adam Coffey
acquisition_process_books.description: While aimed at PE professionals, offers value for acquisition entrepreneurs around value creation and post-acquisition management.

acquisition_process_books.title: Buy Your Business: A Guide to Entrepreneurship through Acquisition
acquisition_process_books.author: Jacob M. Krum
acquisition_process_books.description: Covers choosing the right company for your skillset, finding businesses for sale, due diligence, funding, automating operations, and exit strategy.

acquisition_process_books.title: Acquiring Entrepreneurship
acquisition_process_books.description: Contains stories from 40+ leading business operators, searchers, and investors. Details how to build networks with ETA community members.

operations_management_books.title: The E-Myth Revisited: Why Most Small Businesses Don't Work and What to Do About It
operations_management_books.author: Michael E. Gerber
operations_management_books.description: Classic book on building systems and processes to make small businesses more efficient and scalable. Critical for any acquisition entrepreneur managing a newly acquired business.

operations_management_books.title: Traction: Get a Grip on Your Business
operations_management_books.author: Gino Wickman
operations_management_books.description: Introduces the Entrepreneurial Operating System (EOS). Framework used by many successful operators to effectively manage and grow small businesses.

operations_management_books.title: Built to Sell: Creating a Business That Can Thrive Without You
operations_management_books.author: John Warrillow
operations_management_books.description: Essential for understanding what makes a business attractive to buyers. Useful for building value in an acquired business and preparing for eventual exit.

operations_management_books.title: Good to Great: Why Some Companies Make the Leap... and Others Don't
operations_management_books.author: Jim Collins
operations_management_books.description: Classic business transformation study. Relevant for search fund CEOs taking over and transforming acquired companies.

operations_management_books.title: The First 90 Days: Proven Strategies for Getting Up to Speed Faster and Smarter
operations_management_books.author: Michael D. Watkins
operations_management_books.description: Essential reading for search fund CEOs transitioning into their acquired company. Framework for the critical first months.

operations_management_books.title: Measure What Matters: How Google, Bono, and the Gates Foundation Rock the World with OKRs
operations_management_books.author: John Doerr
operations_management_books.description: OKR framework applicable to managing acquired businesses and setting clear objectives.

ma_valuation_books.title: How to Invest: Masters on the Craft
ma_valuation_books.author: David M. Rubenstein
ma_valuation_books.description: New York Times bestseller with conversations with biggest names in finance. Covers venture capital, real estate, PE, hedge funds, and more.

resource_directories.name: Newton Equity / Search Fund Institute Library

7_legal_tax_advisory_firms.description: Firms that work with or specialize in search fund acquisitions and SME M&A.

united_kingdom.name: Irwin Mitchell
united_kingdom.specialization: Dedicated Search Fund and ETA practice. Years of experience advising on search fund transactions.
united_kingdom.notes: Drinks sponsor of the ETA Awards.

united_kingdom.name: Gerald Edelman
united_kingdom.specialization: Leading reputation in the ETA community. Founders of the ETA Awards. Assists with structuring search vehicles, acquisition 'bidcos', share capital structure, preference share mechanics. Corporate finance advisory.
united_kingdom.notes: Led by Carl Lundberg (CEO and Founder of The ETA Awards).

united_kingdom.name: Buzzacott
united_kingdom.specialization: Financial and tax due diligence, transaction advisory, and tax structuring for search fund and ETA clients. Advised Novastone Capital Advisors on acquisitions.
united_kingdom.notes: Specialist experience from initial review through to completion.

united_kingdom.name: Fladgate LLP
united_kingdom.specialization: Legal advice on search fund transactions. Tax partner (Richard Harbot) with two decades M&A and PE experience. Works with Gerald Edelman's Corporate Finance team.

france.name: Jeausserand Audouard
france.specialization: M&A and tax law. Assists entrepreneurs, managers, investors during complex operations. Recognized for LBO management packages. ~30 lawyers, 10 partners. 30+ corporate operations per year in PE, M&A, tax.
france.notes: Founded 2015. 60 people. Legal 500 ranked for LBO.

spain.name: Uria Menendez
spain.specialization: Leading Spanish M&A firm. Wide range of experience in high-profile M&A deals. Expertise in carve-outs, tender offers, corporate governance. Handled USD 35.68B from 157 deals.

spain.name: Garrigues
spain.specialization: Extensive M&A team with multidisciplinary approach. Industry expertise in renewable energy, telecom, healthcare. Experience in public M&A, takeover bids.
spain.notes: Key partner for banks, PE funds, real estate investors.

spain.name: CMS (Spain/Iberia)
spain.specialization: 45 deals in Iberian M&A transactions in 2024. Cross-border capabilities.

spain.name: Freshfields (Spain)
spain.specialization: M&A and reorganizations. Cross-border mandates coordinating with colleagues in Germany and London. Assists foreign companies entering/exiting Spanish market.

spain.name: Addleshaw Goddard (Spain)
spain.specialization: Tax and structuring expertise for M&A transactions.

germany.name: Vectocon
germany.specialization: Integrated legal and tax advisory from single source for company acquisitions. Specializes in acquisitions for international buyers in Germany. Ensures tax efficiency while maintaining legal certainty.

germany.name: CMS (Germany)
germany.specialization: Tax lawyers in Switzerland and Germany. Broad M&A capabilities.

switzerland.name: Bar & Karrer
switzerland.specialization: Leading position in market for tax issues related to M&A transactions.

switzerland.name: MLL Legal
switzerland.specialization: Leading Swiss tax practice. Offices in Zurich, Zug, Geneva. Advises Swiss and international clients on domestic and international taxation.

switzerland.name: Pestalozzi Attorneys at Law
switzerland.specialization: Expertise in industry-specific tax matters in asset management and investment funds.

switzerland.name: Oberson Abels
switzerland.specialization: Strong presence in French-speaking Switzerland. Advises on tax aspects of M&A and project financing transactions.

pan_european_global.name: Baker McKenzie
pan_european_global.specialization: 1,300+ M&A lawyers in 40+ locations. Market-leading tax and M&A practitioners. Broadest footprint of any law firm.

pan_european_global.name: A&O Shearman
pan_european_global.specialization: Formed 2024 via merger of Allen & Overy and Shearman & Sterling. Top-tier corporate and M&A advice worldwide.

pan_european_global.name: Cleary Gottlieb
pan_european_global.specialization: Multiple Band 1 ranked M&A teams across Europe (Chambers).

pan_european_global.name: KPMG
pan_european_global.specialization: M&A tax advisory including deal advisory, business tax, and transaction structuring.

pan_european_global.name: EY
pan_european_global.specialization: International tax planning. Financial services tax team. Fund tax reporting.

pan_european_global.name: Deloitte
pan_european_global.specialization: Europe's most active financial M&A advisor. Strong M&A teams especially in Germany and Spain.

pan_european_global.name: BDO
pan_european_global.specialization: Mid-market M&A and tax advisory across Europe.

pan_european_global.name: Mazars
pan_european_global.specialization: Mid-market M&A and tax advisory. Strong in France.

pan_european_global.name: RSM
pan_european_global.specialization: M&A tax services. Notable mid-market M&A advisor in Europe.

pan_european_global.name: Alantra
pan_european_global.specialization: Specialist M&A network. Leader in Spanish private equity. Strong mid-market position across Europe.

pan_european_global.name: Lincoln International
pan_european_global.specialization: Global M&A advisory with strong Nordic presence (Stockholm-based Nordic office since 2018). Advisory across all European markets.

pan_european_global.name: Rothschild & Co
pan_european_global.specialization: M&A and strategic advisory. Dedicated mid-market transaction teams including Transaction R&Co in France and Brazil.

us_search_fund_specialists.name: Cooley LLP
us_search_fund_specialists.specialization: Primary fund counsel to 950+ investment fund organizations. 1,200+ funds closed totaling USD 120B+ in commitments. 7,000+ private company clients. PE, M&A, fund formation.

us_search_fund_specialists.name: Shumaker, Loop & Kendrick LLP
us_search_fund_specialists.specialization: 100+ years experience. 300+ attorneys. Corporate, M&A, banking and finance. Toledo, OH headquarters.

7_legal_tax_advisory_firms.notes: For search fund-specific legal and tax work in the UK, Irwin Mitchell, Gerald Edelman, Buzzacott, and Fladgate are the most referenced firms in the ETA community. For larger cross-border transactions, the global Magic Circle and Big 4 firms provide comprehensive support. Many search fund acquisitions in Europe use local boutique firms rather than large international practices due to the smaller deal sizes involved.
